question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: openApiValidator()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to token transfer endpoints with no request validation enforced (validateRequests: false) and craft parameters that cause the mirror node to record an incorrect freeze_status, permanently blocking token transfers for a victim account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: openApiValidator()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ignoreUndocumented: true setting to reach undocumented token-state endpoints that bypass all OpenAPI validation, submitting freeze-related parameters that are never sanitized and cause permanent fund lockout?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: openApiValidator()] Can an unprivileged external user abuse validateRequests: false to inject arbitrary freeze_default=true query parameters into token association requests, causing the mirror node to record all newly associated accounts as frozen by default?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getOpenApiMap()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition during the first initialization of the module-level openApiMap singleton such that a malformed default value for a freeze-related parameter is cached permanently for the lifetime of the process, causing all subsequent token queries to inject a freeze-triggering default?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getOpenApiMap()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that openApiMap is never refreshed after initialization to cause stale freeze-status defaults to persist indefinitely, so that token relationship queries always return FROZEN even after an unfreeze transaction is processed?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getOpenApiParameters()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request path that matches a path in openApiObject.paths whose GET parameters include a $ref pointing to a component with a freeze_default: true default value, causing getOpenApiParameters() to extract and cache that default so it is injected into every subsequent token query?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getOpenApiParameters()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filter (p.in !== 'path') bypass in getOpenApiParameters() to inject a freeze-related query parameter that is not filtered out, causing it to be added as a default to all token endpoint requests and permanently marking accounts as frozen?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getOpenApiParameters()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the defaultValue string coercion ('' + defaultValue) in getOpenApiParameters() to cause a boolean freeze_default value of true to be coerced to the string 'true' and injected as a default query parameter, causing all token queries to filter only frozen accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getV1OpenApiFile()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the module-level v1OpenApiFile singleton caching in getV1OpenApiFile() such that if the spec file is read with corrupted content (e.g., via a symlink race at startup), the corrupted freeze-related defaults are permanently cached and injected into all token requests?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getV1OpenApiFile()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a path traversal via the config.openapi.specFileName value (read from environment variables in config.js) to load an attacker-controlled YAML file as the OpenAPI spec, injecting freeze_default: true as a default parameter that permanently freezes all token accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getSpecPath()] Can an unprivileged external user influence the apiVersion parameter passed to getSpecPath() such that isInValidVersionRange() returns false and the fallback 'v1' path is used, but the actual spec loaded differs from the expected one, causing incorrect freeze-related defaults to be cached?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getSpecPath()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a floating-point apiVersion (e.g., 0.5 or 1.9) that passes the isInValidVersionRange() check (apiVersion > 0 && apiVersion < 2) and causes getSpecPath() to construct a path like 'api/v0.5/openapi.yml' that resolves to an attacker-influenced spec file with freeze-triggering defaults?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: isInValidVersionRange()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a non-integer numeric value (e.g., 1.999) that satisfies apiVersion > 0 && apiVersion < 2 in isInValidVersionRange(), causing getSpecPath() to construct an unexpected spec path that loads a different YAML file with freeze_default: true defaults?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getV1OpenApiObject()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the v1OpenApiDocument singleton in getV1OpenApiObject() by triggering initialization during a window where the YAML spec file contains a freeze-related default value set to true, permanently poisoning the cached document for all subsequent requests?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getOpenApiSpecObject()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit yaml.load() (not yaml.safeLoad()) in getOpenApiSpecObject() to achieve JavaScript prototype pollution via a crafted YAML payload, overwriting the default value of freeze-related parameters on Object.prototype so that all token queries inject freeze_default: true?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getOpenApiSpecObject()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unsafe yaml.load() call in getOpenApiSpecObject() to execute arbitrary JavaScript during YAML deserialization (via js-yaml's !!js/function or !!js/undefined tags), modifying the in-memory openApiMap to inject freeze-triggering defaults for all token endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: serveSpec()] Can an unprivileged external user call the serveSpec() endpoint to retrieve the full OpenAPI YAML spec, extract all default parameter values including freeze-related ones, and use that intelligence to craft requests that exploit the exact default injection behavior to trigger a freeze state on victim accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: serveSwaggerDocs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the externally-loaded customCss URL (camo.githubusercontent.com) in serveSwaggerDocs() to inject malicious CSS or JavaScript into the Swagger UI, tricking legitimate operators into submitting freeze transactions against victim accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: serveSwaggerDocs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the swaggerUi.setup(getV1OpenApiObject(), options) call in serveSwaggerDocs() to cause the Swagger UI to display incorrect freeze_default values, misleading operators into believing accounts are frozen and taking actions that permanently lock funds?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: openApiValidator()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit validateRequests: false to send token freeze-related requests with completely invalid or out-of-range parameters that are never rejected by the middleware, causing downstream handlers to process malformed freeze state changes that permanently lock funds?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getOpenApiMap()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Object.keys(openApiObject.paths).forEach() iteration in getOpenApiMap() to cause a denial-of-service by triggering prototype pollution on openApiObject.paths, injecting extra path entries with freeze-triggering default parameters that affect all token endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getOpenApiParameters()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .map((p) => openApiObject.components.parameters[p]) lookup in getOpenApiParameters() to cause a null-dereference or undefined parameter injection when a $ref points to a non-existent component, causing the normalizer to inject an undefined default that bypasses freeze-status checks?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getOpenApiParameters()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of validation on p.schema?.default in getOpenApiParameters() to cause an object-type default value to be coerced to '[object Object]' via '' + defaultValue, injecting a malformed freeze parameter string that confuses downstream token query handlers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: openApiValidator()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ignoreUndocumented: true to send requests to token endpoints that are not listed in the OpenAPI spec, bypassing all parameter validation and injecting freeze_status=FROZEN parameters that are processed by the database layer without sanitization?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getV1OpenApiFile()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fs.readFileSync() call in getV1OpenApiFile() by influencing the process.cwd() value (e.g., via a symlink or working directory manipulation at startup) to load a malicious YAML spec that sets freeze_default: true as the default for all token association parameters?
